title: Multi-Modal Natural Intelligence through Active Predictive Coding
authors: Jeffrey Duan, Vishwas Sathish, Crimson Stambaugh, Rajesh P. N. Rao
authors_html: Jeffrey Duan, Vishwas Sathish, Crimson Stambaugh, Rajesh P. N. Rao
venue: NeuroAI Multimodal Intelligence Workshop @ AAAI 2026 (PMLR v308)
venue_html: NeuroAI Multimodal Intelligence Workshop @ AAAI 2026 (PMLR v308)
summary: Demonstrates how active predictive coding provides a unified brain-inspired framework for multi-modal intelligence, enabling visual recognition, spatial navigation, language processing, and goal inference through hierarchical sensory-motor circuits.

title: Lessons from Neuroscience for AI: How integrating Actions, Compositional Structure and Episodic Memory could enable Safe, Interpretable and Human-Like AI
authors: Rajesh P. N. Rao, Vishwas Sathish, Linxing Preston Jiang, Matthew Bryan, Prashant Rangarajan
authors_html: Rajesh P. N. Rao, Vishwas Sathish, Linxing Preston Jiang, Matthew Bryan, Prashant Rangarajan
summary: A position paper arguing that integrating actions, compositional structure, and episodic memory (inspired by neuroscience) is key to building safe, interpretable, and human-like AI systems.

title: PainDECOG: Machine Learning-Based Identification of Pain Biomarkers from sEEG Signals
authors: Sidharth Sidharth, Vishwas Sathish, Shweta Bansal, Samantha Sun, Timmy Pham, Kurt Weaver, Rajesh P. N. Rao, Jeffrey Herron
authors_html: Sidharth Sidharth, Vishwas Sathish, Shweta Bansal, Samantha Sun, Timmy Pham, Kurt Weaver, Rajesh P. N. Rao, Jeffrey Herron
summary: Systematic machine-learning approach for classifying acute pain from raw electrophysiological signals, using Power-In-Band features and signal coherence on a curated sEEG dataset.

title: Learning Hierarchical Abstractions of Complex Dynamics using Predictive Coding
authors: Vishwas Sathish, Rajesh P. N. Rao
authors_html: Vishwas Sathish, Rajesh P. N. Rao
venue: Structural Priors Workshop @ Robotics: Science and Systems (RSS) 2024
venue_html: Structural Priors as Inductive Biases for Robot Dynamics Workshop @ RSS 2024
summary: We demonstrate how predictive coding networks can learn hierarchical temporal abstractions of complex dynamical systems, enabling more structured representations of multi-scale dynamics.

title: Active Predictive Coding: A Unifying Neural Model for Active Perception, Compositional Learning, and Hierarchical Planning
authors: Rajesh P. N. Rao, Dimitrios Gklezakos, Vishwas Sathish
authors_html: Rajesh P. N. Rao, Dimitrios Gklezakos, Vishwas Sathish
venue: Neural Computation, 2024  ·  also presented at CogSci 2023
venue_html: Neural Computation, 2024  ·  also presented at CogSci 2023
summary: A unified neural model showing how active predictive coding accounts for active perception, compositional learning, and hierarchical planning in biological and artificial neural systems.

title: Apparatuses, Systems, and Methods for Active Predictive Coding Networks
authors: Rajesh P. N. Rao, Dimitrios Gklezakos, Vishwas Sathish
authors_html: Rajesh P. N. Rao, Dimitrios Gklezakos, Vishwas Sathish
venue: Full US Patent filed (Serial No. 18/408,173), Patent Pending
venue_html: Full US Patent filed (Serial No. 18/408,173), Patent Pending
summary: Patent covering the core architecture and methods of active predictive coding neural networks.

title: Applications of Optimal Stopping Algorithm for Social Graph Based Recommendation
authors: Sai Rohit, Vishwas Sathish, Tanya Mehrotra, Bhaskarjyoti Das
authors_html: Sai Rohit, Vishwas Sathish, Tanya Mehrotra, Bhaskarjyoti Das
venue: IEEE Students Conference on Engineering and Systems (SCES), 2019
summary: Applying optimal stopping theory to improve recommendation accuracy in social network graphs.

title: Graph Embedding Based Hybrid Social Recommendation System
authors: Vishwas Sathish, Tanya Mehrotra, Simran Dhinwa, Bhaskarjyoti Das
authors_html: Vishwas Sathish, Tanya Mehrotra, Simran Dhinwa, Bhaskarjyoti Das
venue: International Conference on Advances in Computing & Communications (ICACC 2019, Oral)
summary: A hybrid recommendation system combining graph embeddings with collaborative filtering for improved social graph-based recommendation.

title: LLeMpower: Understanding Disparities in the Control and Access of Large Language Models
venue: Technical Report, University of Washington, March 2024
summary: Analyzing power disparities and access inequalities in large language model development and deployment.
